explicit_text: My name is Rikae Inouye, and I was named after my grandmother Rika. I was born in Mikemi-town, Nishiwa-county, Ehime-prefecture, and raised in a farming family. I prefer to write my name in katakana because many people use various kanji for it. Throughout my life, I have valued the humble teachings of my grandfather and the moral training I received, especially through learning the tea ceremony from a Buddhist priest starting at age eleven.

explicit_text: I come from Mikemi-town, which is in Nishiwa-county, Ehime-prefecture. It was a farming community where I was raised in a family of farmers, not peasants. Growing up there shaped my appreciation for a humble life and strong family values. The village life involved close ties and respect for farming as a way of life, which my grandfather emphasized often.

explicit_text: I was born and raised in Ehime Prefecture, specifically in Mikemi-town. This region was where my family farmed and lived a humble, rural life. Ehime Prefecture shaped much of my early experiences, including my schooling and religious upbringing in Zen Buddhism. It was also where my family had longstanding ties and where I learned the values that guided me later in life.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in a prefecture on Shikoku island, specifically in a small town in Nishiwa-county. This region was where my family farmed and lived a humble, rural life. This prefecture shaped much of my early experiences, including my schooling and religious upbringing in Zen Buddhism. It was also where my family had longstanding ties and where I learned the values that guided me later in life.

explicit_text: My family practiced Zen Buddhism, and it was the foundation of our religious education. We had temple or shrine officials in the family for generations, and they took care of many community matters. From a young age, I was taught moral lessons rooted in Zen, such as the importance of good manners and humility. This religious background influenced my upbringing deeply, even though I later encountered Christianity after moving to the United States.
implicit_text: My family practiced a form of Buddhism focused on meditation, and it was the foundation of our religious education. We had temple or shrine officials in the family for generations, and they took care of many community matters. From a young age, I was taught moral lessons rooted in this tradition, such as the importance of good manners and humility. This religious background influenced my upbringing deeply, even though I later encountered Christianity after moving to the United States.

explicit_text: Starting at age eleven, I learned the tea ceremony from a Buddhist priest as part of my moral training. It wasn't just about the ceremony itself but about cultivating good manners and humility. I was the youngest in the class, learning alongside adults like the school principal and the village chief's wife. Although we didn't practice tea ceremony at home, it was a significant part of my childhood education and moral development.

explicit_text: I was betrothed to a family involved in sake brewing, a prosperous business at the time. My husband was attending a school of commerce when we married, and I helped keep the books for their store for three years. Unfortunately, their business struggled due to high liquor taxes, which led to financial problems and deception involving bank loans. Despite these hardships, my in-laws were kind to me, though I eventually left the family due to dangerous circumstances.

explicit_text: The Miura family was my husband's family, and they were involved in sake brewing. When their business faced financial difficulties, my grandfather gave his seal to help them secure a bank loan, taking full responsibility. Unfortunately, the Miura family was deceived by others who took advantage of this trust, and rumors spread about our family receiving money, which was untrue. Despite these troubles, the Miura family treated me kindly, but I eventually left due to serious personal dangers.

explicit_text: Though I was raised in a Buddhist family, I came to know about Christianity after moving to the United States. I believe that the Lord Jesus Christ protected me during a dangerous time in my life when I was with my in-laws. Even as a non-believer, I feel that the Lord saved and protected me through love and righteousness. I am grateful to the Lord and feel that His protection has been a guiding force in my life.
